title: Emotional Regulation
period: Development to adulthood: how humans shape emotional states
summary: A practical model of emotions built on arousal, valence, and attention direction, plus the developmental and biological systems that make regulation possible.

context.headline: Emotions are subjective, but they follow patterns
context.description: This module frames emotional regulation as a skill, not a personality trait. Emotions differ from person to person, yet they can be mapped through three dimensions: alert-calm arousal, pleasant-unpleasant valence, and inward-outward attention. Development, hormones, social bonds, and body-brain circuits all shape how well we regulate.
context.when: Infancy, puberty, and everyday adult life
